taskDescription: Drag the correct terms into the blanks to complete statements about the ventricular system and special sensory pathways.
textField:
The lateral ventricles communicate with the 3rd ventricle through the *interventricular* foramen (of Monro), and the 3rd ventricle connects to the 4th ventricle via the *cerebral* aqueduct (of Sylvius). CSF exits the 4th ventricle through the median aperture (foramen of *Magendie*) and two lateral apertures (foramina of *Luschka*).

CSF is produced primarily by the *choroid* plexus in all four ventricles. It is absorbed into the venous system through the *arachnoid* granulations (villi) that project into the superior *sagittal* sinus. The total volume of CSF is approximately *150* mL, and it is produced at a rate of about 500 mL per day.

In obstructive hydrocephalus caused by *aqueductal* stenosis, the lateral and 3rd ventricles are dilated but the *4th* ventricle remains normal. This is because the obstruction is proximal to the 4th ventricle.

At the optic chiasm, *nasal* retinal fibres cross to the opposite side while *temporal* retinal fibres remain ipsilateral. A pituitary tumour compressing the optic chiasm causes *bitemporal* hemianopia due to damage to the crossing nasal fibres from both eyes.

The visual pathway runs from the retina via the optic nerve, chiasm, and *optic* tract to the lateral *geniculate* body of the thalamus, then via the optic radiation to the primary visual cortex in the *occipital* lobe.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the ventricular system and visual pathway.
Good effort! Revise CSF circulation and visual field defects.
Excellent understanding of the ventricular system and sensory pathways!
